---
title: "GIS Project"
format: html
editor: visual
---

## Quarto


```{r}
install.packages(c("cowplot", "hrbrthemes", "knitr", "leaflet", "htmltools", "rosm", "ggspatial", "rnaturalearth", "mapview", "tmap"))
install.packages(c("tidyverse", "sf", "lwgeom", "terra", "stars", "exactextractr"))
install.packages("rinat")
install.packages("viridis")
install.packages("ggplot2")
```

Get the research grade data for the Cape Parrot from iNaturalist
```{r}
library(rinat)

# Get research-grade Cape Parrot observations
cp <- get_inat_obs(taxon_name = "Poicephalus robustus",
                   place_id = 6986,   # South Africa
                   quality = "research",
                   maxresults = 5000)
```

Pul the shapefile of the biomes of Southern Africa
```{r}
# Make sure cp_sf (or cpsf) is an sf object
library(sf)
veg <- st_read("VEGMAP2018_AEA_07012019_beta.Shapefile")
# 1️⃣ Convert your data frame to an sf object
cpsf <- st_as_sf(cp, coords = c("longitude", "latitude"), crs = 4326)
```

Transform the data to fit the map
```{r}
# 2️⃣ Transform the sf object to match your vegetation map CRS
cpsf_proj <- st_transform(cpsf, st_crs(veg))


# Transform the sf object to match the vegetation map CRS
cpsf_proj <- st_transform(cpsf, st_crs(veg))
east_coast_bbox <- st_bbox(c(xmin = 25, ymin = -35, xmax = 35, ymax = -28), crs = st_crs(4326))

# Convert to an sf polygon
east_coast_poly <- st_as_sfc(east_coast_bbox)
east_coast_poly_proj <- st_transform(east_coast_poly, st_crs(veg))

veg_cropped <- st_intersection(veg, east_coast_poly_proj)

# Look for a field like 'VEG_CODE', 'VEG_TYPE', or similar
library(viridis)

# Example bounding box (lon/lat) for east coast of South Africa
east_coast_bbox <- st_bbox(c(xmin = 25, ymin = -35, xmax = 35, ymax = -15), crs = st_crs(4326))

# Convert bbox to an sf polygon
east_coast_poly <- st_as_sfc(east_coast_bbox)

# Transform the bbox to the CRS of your vegetation map
east_coast_poly_proj <- st_transform(east_coast_poly, st_crs(veg))  # create named vector

# Crop using intersection
veg_cropped <- st_intersection(veg, east_coast_poly_proj)
# Remove NA biome rows
#TAKES FOREVR

# Drop unused factor levels (important)
veg_cropped_clean$BIOME_18 <- droplevels(as.factor(veg_cropped_clean$BIOME_18))

# Make sure it's treated as a factor
veg_cropped$BIOME_18 <- as.factor(veg_cropped$BIOME_18)
biomes <- unique(veg_cropped$BIOME_18)
colors <- viridis(length(biomes))
biome_colors <- setNames(colors, biomes)
```

```{r}
plot(st_geometry(veg_cropped), col = biome_colors[veg_cropped$BIOME_18],
     border = NA, main = "Vegetation Map with Cape Parrot Sitings")

cpsf <- st_as_sf(cp, coords = c("longitude", "latitude"), crs = 4326)
cpsf_proj <- st_transform(cpsf, st_crs(veg))

plot(veg_cropped["BIOME_18"], pal = viridis, border = NA, main = "Vegetation Map with Cape Parrot Sitings")

# Add Cape Parrot points (already transformed to veg CRS)
plot(st_geometry(cpsf_proj), add = TRUE, col = "red", pch = 19, cex = 0.4)
legend("topright",
       legend = biomes,
       fill = colors,   # must be the vector of colors used in plot
       border = NA,
       cex = 0.7,
       bty = "n")
```

```{r}
cpsf <- st_as_sf(cp, coords = c("longitude", "latitude"), crs = 4326)
cpsf_proj <- st_transform(cpsf, st_crs(veg))
parrot_bbox <- st_bbox(cpsf_proj)
parrot_bbox_poly <- st_as_sfc(parrot_bbox)
parrot_bbox_buffer <- st_buffer(parrot_bbox_poly, dist = 50000)
veg_crop_parrot <- st_intersection(veg, st_union(parrot_buffer)) # 50 km

veg_crop_parrot <- st_intersection(veg, parrot_buffer)
veg_crop_parrot <- veg_crop_parrot[!is.na(veg_crop_parrot$BIOME_18), ]
veg_crop_parrot$BIOME_18 <- droplevels(as.factor(veg_crop_parrot$BIOME_18))

plot(veg_crop_parrot["BIOME_18"],
     pal = viridis,
     border = NA,
     main = "Cape Parrot Sightings by Biome")

plot(st_geometry(cpsf_proj), add = TRUE, col = "red", pch = 19, cex = 0.3)




veg_crop_parrot <- st_crop(veg, parrot_bbox_buffer)
veg_crop_parrot <- veg_crop_parrot[!is.na(veg_crop_parrot$BIOME_18), ]
veg_crop_parrot$BIOME_18 <- droplevels(as.factor(veg_crop_parrot$BIOME_18))
veg_crop_parrot <- veg_crop_parrot[!is.na(veg_crop_parrot$BIOME_18), ]
veg_crop_parrot$BIOME_18 <- droplevels(as.factor(veg_crop_parrot$BIOME_18))
library(viridis)


cpsf_proj <- st_transform(cpsf, st_crs(veg_crop_parrot))
st_bbox(cpsf_proj)
st_bbox(veg_crop_parrot)
cpsf_proj <- st_transform(cpsf_proj, st_crs(veg_crop_parrot))


st_crs(cpsf_proj)$epsg
st_crs(veg_crop_parrot)$epsg






# Make sure points are projected to vegetation CRS
cpsf_proj <- st_transform(cpsf_prof, st_crs(veg_crop_parrot))

# Now plot
plot(veg_crop_parrot["BIOME_18"],
     pal = viridis,
     border = NA,
     main = "Cape Parrot Sightings by Eastern South Africa")

plot(st_geometry(cpsf),
     add = TRUE,
     col = "red",
     pch = 19,
     cex = 0.6)
```


```{r}
#COMEON
# Ensure CRS matches
cpsf_proj <- st_transform(cpsf, st_crs(veg))

# Crop vegetation to parrot buffer
parrot_bbox <- st_bbox(cpsf_proj)
parrot_buffer <- st_buffer(st_as_sfc(parrot_bbox), 50000)
veg_crop <- st_crop(veg, parrot_buffer)

# Remove NA biomes
veg_crop <- veg_crop[!is.na(veg_crop$BIOME_18), ]
veg_crop$BIOME_18 <- droplevels(as.factor(veg_crop$BIOME_18))


# IMPORTANT: Plot only points inside cropped area
cpsf_inside <- cpsf_proj[st_intersects(cpsf_proj, veg_crop, sparse = FALSE), ]

plot(cpsf_inside,
     add = TRUE,
     col = "red",
     pch = 20,
     cex = 0.4)



library(ggplot2)
ggplot() +
  geom_sf(data = veg_crop, aes(fill = BIOME_18), color = NA) +
  geom_sf(data = cpsf_proj, color = "red", size = 1) +
  scale_fill_viridis_d(name = "Biome") +
  labs(
    title = "Cape Parrot Sightings by Biome",
    subtitle = "South Africa",
    x = NULL,
    y = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "right"
  )
```
